notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 via read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes). Agent read SKILL.md and graded all 3 eval scenarios against documented behavior. All 3 evals scored 1.0 — the skill specification fully covers all tested flows.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: navigates to the target app and feature area
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 2 requires navigating to the app and identifying the feature area before any testing begins.

assertions.id: performs CRUD operations and verifies each step
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 3 requires exercising CRUD paths with verification at each step — create, read, update, delete.

assertions.id: produces a structured test report with pass/fail per scenario
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 5 defines the structured report format with pass/fail status per tested scenario.

assertions.id: does NOT spawn parallel agents in single mode
assertions.note: SKILL.md explicitly states single-mode runs sequentially — parallel agent spawning is reserved for multi-perspective mode.

runs.notes: Single-mode flow is the baseline path. All 4 expectations are directly mapped to SKILL.md steps.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: SKILL.md multi-perspective mode: spawns one agent per persona (6 Dutch government user archetypes) concurrently.

assertions.id: launches agents in parallel rather than sequentially
assertions.note: SKILL.md explicitly requires parallel launch for multi-perspective mode — sequential would defeat the purpose of perspective coverage.

assertions.id: each agent produces independent findings from its persona's viewpoint
assertions.note: Each persona agent operates independently; findings reflect that persona's use context and expectations.

assertions.id: synthesizes a combined report across all persona findings
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 5 in multi-perspective mode requires synthesizing all persona reports into a unified report with cross-persona patterns noted.

runs.notes: Multi-perspective mode is the differentiated path. All 4 expectations map to SKILL.md multi-perspective steps.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: loads scenario definitions from the scenarios file
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 1 requires loading the scenarios file and parsing scenario IDs and their definitions.

assertions.id: filters to only TS-003 and TS-004 when those IDs are requested
assertions.note: SKILL.md scenario-filter logic: when specific IDs are passed, only those scenarios are run — others are skipped entirely.

assertions.id: surfaces TS-001 and TS-002 as skipped, not as failures
assertions.note: Skipped scenarios are reported as 'not run' in the output, not as failures — SKILL.md report format distinguishes skip from fail.

assertions.id: asks user before running if scenario list is ambiguous
assertions.note: SKILL.md uses AskUserQuestion when the requested scenario IDs do not clearly resolve — confirms before proceeding.

assertions.id: reports results per scenario ID in the output
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 5 report format groups results by scenario ID so each scenario's outcome is individually traceable.

runs.notes: Scenario-filtering is the precision path. All 5 expectations are fully specified in SKILL.md's scenario-handling steps.
